title: Yorker
answer: A yorker is a very full delivery that lands at or near the batter's feet or the base of the stumps. It is hard to score from cleanly and is one of the most effective balls in death overs.

title: Googly
answer: A googly is the leg-spinner's wrong'un. It looks like a normal leg-break out of the hand, but it turns the other way after pitching.

title: Doosra
answer: A doosra is an off-spinner's variation that turns away from a right-handed batter instead of turning in like a normal off-break. It is designed to surprise the batter by changing the expected direction of spin.

title: Maiden Over
answer: A maiden over is an over in which the bowler concedes no runs off the bat or extras. It shows tight, disciplined bowling pressure.

title: Hat-Trick
answer: A hat-trick happens when a bowler takes three wickets with three consecutive legal deliveries. Those balls can span two overs if the bowler finishes one over and starts the next.
examples: What is a hat-trick in cricket?

title: Test vs ODI vs T20
answer: Test cricket is the longest format and can last up to five days with two innings per side. ODIs are 50 overs per side and usually finish in one day. T20 matches are 20 overs per side and are designed to be the shortest and fastest-scoring international format.

title: Overs Breakdown
answer: A T20 innings is 20 overs. An ODI innings is 50 overs. Test cricket does not have a fixed overs cap per innings, although each day has a target overs quota for the fielding side.
examples: How many overs are in ODI?
